items.passage: Pollination is the process by which flowering plants exchange pollen between flowers to make seeds. Bees, butterflies, and small birds often visit bright flowers to drink sweet nectar. As they feed, soft grains of pollen stick to their bodies and travel to the next flower. The next plant uses this pollen to grow tiny fruits and new seeds.

Soil erosion happens when wind and rain carry away the top layer of dirt that helps plants grow. Heavy storms can move a lot of loose soil into nearby rivers and lakes. Strong roots from trees and grass hold the ground in place during such storms. Without these roots, farmland may slowly turn into hard, dusty land that few crops can use.

Attention is the way the brain picks out one thing to think about while it pushes other things into the background. In a noisy room, people often focus on one voice and barely hear the rest. Bright lights, loud sounds, and quick motion grab attention fast, even when we try to ignore them. Good rest and short breaks help most people stay focused longer.

The Silk Road was a long network of trade routes that linked China with parts of Europe more than two thousand years ago. Camels carried heavy bags of silk, spice, and tea across high mountains and dry deserts. Merchants traded goods at many small towns along the way and shared new ideas about food, music, and faith. These long trips helped many distant cultures learn from one another.

A geyser is a hot spring that shoots water and steam high into the sky every so often. Cool rain water sinks into cracks in the ground and slowly heats up near hot rock far below. Once the pressure grows too strong, the water bursts upward as a tall jet of spray. Many of the most famous geysers in the world sit inside Yellowstone National Park.

A nebula is a large cloud of dust and gas that floats in deep space between the stars. Some clouds glow with bright colors when light from nearby stars heats the gas inside them. Others stay dark and quietly hide behind small bands of stardust. Many young stars are born inside these clouds when small parts slowly pull together under their own weight.
